question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the BLS on April 10, 2026. According to the official StockPil report, the CPI-U increased 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis, which is significantly below the 0.8% threshold needed for YES resolution. The market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty since the actual data shows only 0.3% monthly increase.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased only 0.3% month-over-month
0.3% is well below the 0.8% threshold required for YES resolution
Market closes April 10, 2026 - same day the data was released
This is now a binary outcome based on published government statistics
risks: Data revision risk is minimal as BLS CPI revisions are rare and small
Market may not have fully updated to reflect the released data yet
